engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Impreza Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Impreza in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru Impreza EJ205 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ringland failure - piston ring lands crack under high boost, causing oil consumption and compression loss.
Head gasket failure - common on high-mileage turbo boxer engines, typically 70k-100k miles.
Turbocharger wear - boost loss and oil consumption common after 80k miles on high-performance units.

cards.altText: Subaru Impreza FB20 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil use on higher-mileage naturally aspirated units.
Timing chain stretch - chain tensioner wear can cause rattling on engines over 90k miles.
Valve cover gasket leaks - common ageing issue on SOHC boxer configuration.

cards.altText: Subaru Impreza EJ207 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ringland failure - documented across high-output EJ207 units, typically under track or aggressive driving.
Head gasket failure - turbo heat cycles accelerate gasket degradation on high-performance boxer engines.
Turbocharger wear - IHI VF series turbos require vigilant servicing to maintain boost performance.

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Turbo

commonFailures: Ringland failure - piston ring lands crack under high boost, typically 60k-90k miles

Turbocharger wear - boost loss and oil consumption common after 80k miles

commonFailures: Head gasket failure - common on high-mileage naturally aspirated boxer engines

Timing belt service critical - interference engine, belt failure causes catastrophic damage

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Impreza Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Impreza's EJ and FB series boxer engines deliver engaging performance but share documented failure points with other Subaru models. Ringland failure dominates replacement enquiries on turbo models, followed by head gasket issues and oil consumption concerns. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Ringland Failure - Why Subaru Impreza Turbo Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Impreza WRX, WRX STI (2001-2014, EJ205/EJ207/EJ255/EJ257)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring lands crack under high boost pressure and heat cycles, allowing oil to pass into combustion chambers and causing compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Complex labour; viable only if block/heads in excellent condition

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EJ turbo engine with upgraded rings, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month unlimited warranty, addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru Impreza WRX or STI worth £5,000-£15,000, a full rebuilt turbo engine often represents better value than a ring-only repair, especially if the vehicle has been tracked or driven aggressively. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded forged components to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ turbo engine prices for your Subaru Impreza

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Subaru Impreza EJ Series Coolant/Oil Cross-Contamination
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Impreza WRX, WRX STI, 2.0R (2000-2014, EJ201/EJ202/EJ205/EJ207/EJ255/EJ257)
problems.rootCause: Original head gaskets degrade under heat cycles and boxer engine vibration, allowing coolant and oil to mix, causing overheating and potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace gasket with upgraded MLS unit, reseal, test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/heads undamaged; 30-50k mile expectation on gasket

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EJ engine with MLS gaskets, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses head gasket + common EJ failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Impreza has high mileage or shows signs of coolant/oil mixing beyond the head gasket, a full rebuilt EJ engine typically costs less than separate gasket repair + ancillary work. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded MLS gaskets.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ engine prices for your Subaru Impreza

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Impreza WRX/STI Boost Loss & Oil Consumption
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Impreza WRX, WRX STI (2001-2014, EJ205/EJ207/EJ255/EJ257)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings and seals wear under boost pressure and heat, causing oil leakage into intake/exhaust and loss of boost performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild or replace with reconditioned unit, replace lines/gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block healthy; 40-60k mile expectation on turbo

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EJ Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EJ turbo engine with new/reconditioned turbo, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses turbo + common EJ failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Impreza WRX or STI has high mileage or shows signs of oil consumption beyond the turbo, a full rebuilt EJ turbo engine often costs less than separate turbo + head gasket repairs. We recommend rebuilt units with turbo health guarantees.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ turbo engine prices for your Subaru Impreza

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - High-Mileage FB20 Piston Ring Wear
problems.rootCause: Piston ring lands wear on ageing boxer engines, allowing oil to pass into combustion chambers and burn.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Complex labour; viable only if block/heads in excellent condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt FB20 engine with updated piston rings, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective long-term solution; eliminates oil consumption root cause

problems.recommendation: For Impreza owners experiencing excessive oil use beyond 80k miles, a rebuilt FB20 engine with updated piston rings typically offers better value than a ring-only repair, especially on modern boxer engines.
problems.cta: Get quotes for low-oil-consumption rebuilt Impreza engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Impreza
finalCta.paragraph: Ringland, head gasket, turbo, or oil consumption issues don't have to mean the end for your Impreza. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru Impreza specialists who understand boxer engine compatibility across EJ and FB series. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Impreza Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Impreza and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru Impreza Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Subaru Impreza in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Subaru Impreza 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre EJ16/FB16 boxer family powered entry-level Subaru Impreza models in the UK, offering economical naturally aspirated performance for budget-conscious buyers. This compact SOHC flat-four design provides smooth power delivery and AWD compatibility, though head gasket vigilance is essential for long-term reliability.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket degradation on high-mileage units; oil consumption on higher-mileage naturally aspirated examples
items.cta: Compare Subaru Impreza 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Impreza 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre EJ20/FB20 boxer family powered the majority of Subaru Impreza models sold in the UK, from economical 2.0R variants to the performance-focused WRX. This SOHC/DOHC flat-four design with turbo or naturally aspirated options offers versatile power delivery and AWD compatibility, though ringland and head gasket vigilance is essential for turbo models.
commonFailurePoints: Ringland failure on turbo EJ205/EJ207; head gasket degradation on all EJ series; oil consumption on high-mileage FB20 units
items.cta: Compare Subaru Impreza 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Impreza 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The enlarged 2.5-litre EJ255/EJ257 turbo boxer defined later Subaru Impreza WRX and WRX STI models from 2005, delivering increased displacement and torque for improved drivability while retaining the engaging turbocharged character enthusiasts expect.
commonFailurePoints: Ringland failure on high-output turbo units; head gasket degradation on high-mileage 2.5L turbo boxer engines; oil consumption on higher-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Subaru Impreza 2.5L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Subaru Impreza needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Subaru Impreza specialists.

items.title: Subaru Impreza Petrol Engines
items.description: Every Subaru Impreza sold in the UK uses a petrol boxer engine, with naturally aspirated EJ16/EJ20/FB20 units for base models and turbocharged EJ205/EJ207/EJ255/EJ257 for performance WRX/STI variants.
items.descriptor: Every Subaru Impreza sold in the UK uses a petrol boxer engine, with naturally aspirated EJ16/EJ20/FB20 units for base models and turbocharged EJ205/EJ207/EJ255/EJ257 for performance WRX/STI variants.
items.familiesLabel: Common Subaru Impreza Petrol Engine Families
families: EJ205 - First-gen WRX turbo boxer, most common replacement engine for 2001-2010 performance models
EJ207 - STI-spec turbo boxer delivering 276-300hp, high-demand for enthusiast and track-focused Impreza owners
FB20 - Modern chain-driven naturally aspirated boxer, improved reliability and reduced maintenance for 2012+ models
foundIn: First Generation (2000-2007) - EJ16/EJ20 naturally aspirated and turbo
Third Generation (2012-present) - FB20 naturally aspirated, FA20F turbo (limited UK)
knownFor: Engaging boxer performance with linear power delivery and AWD traction
Turbo models require vigilant servicing to avoid ringland and head gasket issues
Compatibility with Legacy/Forester EJ/FB donor engines simplifies sourcing for UK replacements

engineYears.h2: Subaru Impreza Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Impreza's 2000-present production run, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their EJ/FB engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices.

years.description: The launch years introduced the EJ16/EJ20 SOHC boxer to UK Impreza models, establishing the reliable but head-gasket-sensitive platform shared with Legacy and Forester.
keyChanges: Subaru Impreza launched for UK market with EJ16/EJ20 naturally aspirated boxer engines
WRX performance variant introduced with EJ205 turbo boxer delivering 220-265hp
Head gasket recall campaign issued for early EJ20 series units
Manual and automatic transmission options with different ECU mapping
mainEngines: EJ201/EJ202 - 2.0L petrol naturally aspirated (2.0R, GX, RX)
knownFor: Economical naturally aspirated boxer performance for UK buyers
Head gasket vigilance essential from 70k miles onwards on EJ series
Strong compatibility with Legacy/Forester EJ donor engines for replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2007 Subaru Impreza replacement engines
years.ticker: Impreza head gasket replacement • EJ205 turbo engine supply UK • WRX boxer engine compatibility

years.description: The second generation brought refined turbo boxer engines to UK Impreza WRX and WRX STI models, with increased displacement options and improved reliability over early production.
keyChanges: Hatchback body style introduced alongside saloon for 2007 model year
EJ255 2.5L turbo boxer introduced for WRX variants from 2005-2014
EJ207 STI-spec turbo boxer continued for performance flagship models
Minor ECU and fuel system refinements for improved driveability across all trims
knownFor: EJ255 refinements improved low-end torque for UK road conditions
WRX STI offered enhanced performance for enthusiast and track-focused buyers
Low-mileage 2012-2014 examples command premium in UK enthusiast market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2014 Subaru Impreza replacement engines
years.ticker: Impreza EJ255 ringland replacement • WRX STI turbo compatibility • 2014 Impreza low mileage engine

years.preview: Present - Third Generation Impreza: FB20 Introduction
years.description: The third generation brought the modern FB20 chain-driven boxer to UK Impreza 2.0i models, improving reliability and reducing maintenance requirements for daily drivers.
keyChanges: All-new platform and styling for 2012 model year
FB20 2.0L naturally aspirated chain-driven boxer introduced across all non-turbo trims
Timing chain replaced timing belt, reducing long-term maintenance costs
Improved fuel economy and emissions compliance for UK market requirements
mainEngines: FB20 - 2.0L petrol naturally aspirated (2.0i, Sport, SE, GT)
knownFor: FB20 delivers improved reliability with chain-driven design for daily drivers
Reduced maintenance requirements compared to belt-driven EJ series
Modern examples still under warranty; replacement demand growing as mileage accumulates
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012+ Subaru Impreza replacement engines
years.ticker: Impreza FB20 oil consumption • Third gen boxer compatibility • 2017+ Impreza replacement options

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Subaru Impreza was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Subaru Impreza specialists.

faq.h2: Subaru Impreza Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Impreza engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Impreza engine replacements typically range from £1,200 for rebuilt FB20 units to £4,500 for rebuilt EJ207 STI engines, supply only. Supply & fit adds £550-£1,100 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Naturally aspirated EJ201/EJ202/FB20 rebuilt: £1,200-£2,800 supply only
Turbo EJ205/EJ255 rebuilt: £1,895-£3,800 supply only
STI-spec EJ207/EJ257 rebuilt: £2,400-£4,500 supply only
All quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get your free Subaru Impreza engine replacement quote today

items.question: Which engine code fits my Subaru Impreza?
items.answer: Your Impreza uses either EJ16/EJ20 (naturally aspirated), EJ205/EJ207/EJ255/EJ257 (turbo) or FB20 (modern NA) depending on variant and year. Enter your registration or VIN above to instantly verify your exact engine code.
keyPoints: 1.6R/2.0R models (2000-2007): EJ16/EJ201/EJ202 naturally aspirated
WRX models (2001-2014): EJ205/EJ255 turbocharged
WRX STI models (2001-2014): EJ207/EJ257 high-output turbo
2.0i models (2012+): FB20 chain-driven naturally aspirated
items.cta: Verify your Impreza engine code with our free compatibility checker

items.question: What is the difference between used and reconditioned Impreza engines?
items.answer: Used engines are tested second-hand units from breakers, while reconditioned engines are fully rebuilt with new bearings, seals and upgraded components. Reconditioned units include longer warranties and address common failure points.
keyPoints: Used: £950-£3,200, 6-month warranty typical, mileage varies
Reconditioned: £1,200-£4,500, 12-month unlimited warranty standard
Reconditioned includes upgraded rings and MLS head gaskets
Reconditioned recommended for high-mileage or failure-damaged units
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru Impreza engine options

items.question: How long does Impreza engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Most Subaru Impreza engines are dispatched next-day from UK stock; fitting takes 1-2 days at approved Subaru specialists. Total turnaround from quote to road-ready is typically 3-5 working days.
keyPoints: Engines in UK stock: dispatched next working day
Specialist fitting: 1-2 days at approved Subaru garage
Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours
EJ/FB compatibility checks completed before dispatch
items.cta: Check live Subaru Impreza engine availability and fitting slots

items.question: Do Impreza replacement engines come with warranty in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, all Subaru Impreza engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Turbo EJ207 models may qualify for extended performance warranty upgrades.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage
Performance models eligible for extended coverage
Claim process managed directly through supplying specialist
items.cta: View warranty options for your Subaru Impreza engine replacement

items.question: Can I fit a different EJ engine code in my Impreza?
items.answer: EJ201 and EJ202 are often interchangeable with ECU verification, but EJ205/EJ207 turbo requires matching wiring and mounts. Use our compatibility checker or speak to a Subaru Impreza specialist before ordering a different code.
keyPoints: EJ201 and EJ202 often interchangeable on naturally aspirated models
EJ205/EJ207 requires matching ECU, wiring harness and fuel system
Mounting points and AWD compatibility must be confirmed
Specialist advice recommended for cross-code swaps on performance models
items.cta: Get expert advice on EJ code compatibility for your Impreza

items.question: Will a replacement Impreza engine pass MOT emissions in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, all replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket comply with UK MOT emissions standards. Documentation is provided to support compliance testing if required.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines tested to meet original emissions specifications
Documentation provided for MOT compliance
EJ/FB series engines retain original emissions classification
Specialists assist with testing paperwork where needed
items.cta: Ensure your Impreza replacement engine is MOT-compliant

items.answer: Use our garage locator to find approved Subaru Impreza engine specialists near your postcode. All fitters are vetted, experienced with boxer engines and recognise our network warranty.

All garages experienced with EJ/FB boxer and AWD systems
Warranty recognised at any approved fitting location
Supply & fit quotes include labour at your chosen garage

items.question: What if my Impreza engine replacement doesn't fit?
items.answer: All Subaru Impreza engines come with a compatibility guarantee. If the engine doesn't fit your vehicle, we'll replace it with the correct unit or refund you in full, no questions asked.
keyPoints: Compatibility guarantee covers all engine code mismatches
Free replacement or full refund if fitment issues arise
Pre-dispatch compatibility checks minimise risk
Specialist support available throughout installation process
items.cta: Order your Impreza engine with complete fitment confidence

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Subaru Impreza in the UK?
items.answer: For most Impreza owners, engine replacement costs £1,200-£4,500 versus £5,000-£20,000+ for a replacement vehicle. Rebuilt engines extend life with upgraded components and warranty protection.
keyPoints: Replacement cost typically far below vehicle market value
Upgraded components address original failure points
12-month warranty provides peace of mind for enthusiast vehicles
Preserve a unique vehicle with strong enthusiast following
items.cta: Calculate cost-benefit for your Subaru Impreza engine replacement
